questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: When a glass prism of refracting angle $60^{\circ}$ is immersed in a liquid its angle of minimum deviation is $30^{\circ}$. The critical angle of glass with respect to the liquid medium is
questions.solution:
$A=60^{\circ}, \delta=30^{\circ}$
Refractive index of prism with respect to liquid,
$\begin{aligned} \mu & =\frac{\sin \frac{A+\delta_n}{2}}{\sin \frac{A}{2}}=\frac{\sin \frac{\left(60^{\circ}+30^{\circ}\right)}{2}}{\sin \frac{60^{\circ}}{2}} \\ & =\frac{\sin 45^{\circ}}{\sin 30^{\circ}}=\frac{\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}{\frac{1}{2}}=\sqrt{2}\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Critical angle $=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\mu}\right)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)$
$=\sin ^{-1}\left(\sin 45^{\circ}\right)=45^{\circ}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two similar thin equi-convex lenses of focal length <math><mi>f</mi></math> each are kept coaxially in contact with each other, such that the focal length of the combination is <math><msub><mrow><mi>F</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>.</mo></math> When the space between the two lenses is filled with glycerin (which has the same refractive index (<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>μ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1.5</mn></math>) as that of glass), then the equivalent focal length is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>F</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>.</mo></math> The ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>F</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>:</mo><msub><mi>F</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> will be
questions.solution:
Case I:

1Feq+1F+1F
F1=Feq=F2
Case II:

F2=Feq=F
F1:F2=1:2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The image of an object O due to reflection from the surface of a lake is elongated due to the ripples on the water surface caused by a light breeze. This is because the ripples act as tilted mirrors as shown. Consider the case where O and the observer E are at the same height above the surface of the lake. If the maximum angle that the ripples make with the horizontal is σ, the angular extent δ of the image will be,
questions.solution:
In trapezium OECB, the sides OE∥CB and the angles are shown in the figure below:

 

x=90°-u-α

y=90°-v-α

∠OPB=∠EPC 

∠OPE=∠BOC

which gives, 90°-u-α=90°-v-α

⇒u=v

∠OPE+∠EPC=180°

180°-x+y+180-2v-δ=180°

-180°-u-v-2α+180°-2v-δ=0

(u=v)

⇒δ=2α.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Match List I (Phenomenon) with List II (Principle) and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists.

  List I   List II
(I) Blue colour of a sky (A) Total internal reflection
(II) Glittering of diamond (B) Dispersion of light
(III) Formation of rainbow (C) Scattering of light
(IV) In the evening when the sun goes down below the horizon, it continues to remain visible for some time (D) Refraction of light
questions.solution:
I As sunlight enters the Earth's atmosphere, all the gas molecules and dust in the atmosphere scatter the white light in every direction.  Since blue light moves as shorter, smaller waves it scatters more than other colours. Thus, the blue colour of the sky is due to the scattering of light.

II As light strikes the diamond, it refracts within the lattice and flows through it. At different angles, total internal reflection happens, and the light gradually refracts out. The diamond's shape causes light to strike at an angle higher than the critical angle, causing it to reflect back. Thus, the glittering of the diamond is due to total internal reflection.

III A rainbow is a meteorological effect that happens as sunlight is reflected, refracted, and dispersed in water droplets, resulting in a spectrum of light visible in the sky. Thus, the formation of rainbow is due to the dispersion of light.

IV Because of atmospheric refraction of light, we can see the rising sun before the actual sunrise and then after the actual sunset. The density of air near the earth's surface is greater. The sun's rays travel through space to penetrate a denser medium, where they bend gradually towards normal with each refraction and become visible to us before the real sunrise or after sunset.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A fish is near the centre of a spherical fishbowl filled with water (<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">w</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mfrac bevelled="true"><mn>4</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math>). A child is standing at a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>R</mi></math> from the centre of the bowl, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is the radius of curvature of the bowl. The distance from the centre of the fishbowl, where the child's nose will appear to the fish is
questions.solution:
μ 2 v - μ 1 u = μ 2 - μ 1 R ⇒ 4 3 v - 1 - R = 4 3 - 1 R ⇒ 4 3 v = 1 3 R - 1 R

4 3 v = 1 - 3 3 R ⇒ v = - 4 R 2 ⇒ v = - 2 R

Distance of image from centre = 3R
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The two surfaces of a biconvex lens has same radii of curvatures. This lens is made of glass of refractive index 1.5 and has a focal length $10 \mathrm{~cm}$ in air. The lens is cut into two equal halves along a plane perpendicular to its principal axis to yield two plano-convex lenses. The two pieces are glued such that the convex surfaces touch each other. If this combination lens is immersed in water (refractive index $=$ $4 / 3$ ), its focal length (in $\mathrm{cm}$ ) is :
questions.solution:
If $a$ lens of focal length $f$ is divided into two equal parts as shown in figure (1) and each has a focal length $f^{\prime}$ then $\frac{1}{f}=\frac{1}{f^{\prime}}+\frac{1}{f^{\prime}} \quad \text { i.e., } f^{\prime}=2 f$ i.e., each part will have focal length $2 f$. Now if these parts are put in contact as in figure (2), then resultant focal length of the combination will be ' $\frac{1}{F}=\frac{1}{2 f}+\frac{1}{2 f} \text { i.e., } F=f \quad \text { (initial value) }$
For this combination, $\frac{1}{F}=\left({ }_a \mu_g-1\right)\left(\frac{1}{R_1}-\frac{1}{R_2}\right)$ $\ldots$ (i) Now, if this combination is immersed in liquid, then $\frac{1}{F^{\prime}}=\left({ }_l \mu_g-1\right)\left(\frac{1}{R_1}-\frac{1}{R_2}\right)$ $\ldots$ (ii) $\therefore \frac{F^{\prime}}{f}=\frac{\left({ }_a \mu_g-1\right)}{\left.{ }_l \mu_g-1\right)}=\frac{(1.5-1)}{\left(\frac{\frac{3}{2}}{\frac{4}{3}}-1\right)}$ or $\frac{F^{\prime}}{f}=\frac{0.5}{\left(\frac{9}{8}-1\right)}=0.5 \times 8$ $\therefore F^{\prime}=0.5 \times 8 \times 10=40 \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: It is desired to make a converging achromatic combination of mean focal length <math> <mrow> <mn>50</mn><mtext> </mtext><mtext>cm</mtext></mrow> </math> by using two lenses of materials <math><mi>A</mi></math> and <math><mi>B</mi></math>. If the dispersive power of <math><mi>A</mi></math> and <math><mi>B</mi></math> are in ratio <math> <mrow> <mn>1</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo></mrow> </math> the focal lengths of the convex and the concave lenses are respectively
questions.solution:
ω1ω2=12
Now, f1f2=-ω1ω2=-12
Or  f2=-2f1
Now, 1F=1f1+1f2
150=1f1+1-2f1
Or 50f=-2+1-2f1=12f1
Or 2f1=50 or f1=25 cm
Again f2=−2×25 cm
f2=−50 cm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The magnifying power of a telescope is nine. When it is adjusted for parallel rays, the distance between the objective and eyepiece is 20 cm. The focal length of objective and eyepiece are respectively.
questions.solution:
For final image at infinity, magnifying power of a telescope is given by
m=fofe=9
where, m = magnification,
andfo = focal length of eyepiece
⇒fo=9fe …. (i)
Also, distance between objective and eyepiece
=fo+fe=20 (given)
⇒9fe+fe=20⇒fe=2cm
fo=9fe=18cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A circular beam of light of diameter <math><mi>d</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> falls on a plane surface of glass. The angle of incidence is <math><msup><mrow><mn>60</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></mrow></msup></math> and refractive index of glass is <math><mi>μ</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></math>. The diameter of the refracted beam is
questions.solution:
From the diagram, diameter of incident beam is d1=PQ cos60o and diameter of refracted beam is d2=PQcosr.
∴ d1d2=12cosr
⇒d2=2d1cosr 

From Snell's law:
1×sini=μsinr
sinr=sin60°μ=13
⇒cosr=23
∴ d2=2×2×23=3.26 cm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A ray of light refracts from medium 1 into a thin layer of medium 2, crosses the layer and is incident at the critical angle on the interface between the medium 2 and 3 as shown in the figure. If the angle of incidence of ray is $\theta$, the value of $\theta$ is
questions.solution:
As the light ray incident at critical angle,
$\begin{aligned}
& \quad \sin C=\frac{\mu_3}{\mu_2}=\frac{1.3}{1.8} \text { and } C=r \\
& \therefore \quad \sin r=\frac{1.3}{1.8} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \sin \theta=\frac{1.8}{1.6} \times \frac{1.3}{1.8}=\frac{13}{16} \\
& \theta=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{13}{16}\right)
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A convex lens of focal length $0.15 \mathrm{~m}$ is made of a material of refractive index $\frac{3}{2}$. When it is placed in a liquid, its focal length is increased by $0.225 \mathrm{~m}$. The refractive index of the liquid is
questions.solution:
$$
f_a=0.15 \mathrm{~m}, \mu_g=3 / 2, f_e=0.225+0.15
$$
From lens formula,
For air, $\frac{1}{f_a}=\left(\frac{\mu_g}{\mu_a}-1\right)\left(\frac{1}{R_1}-\frac{1}{R_2}\right)$
For liquid, $\frac{1}{f_l}=\left(\frac{\mu_g}{\mu_l}-1\right)\left(\frac{1}{R_1}-\frac{1}{R_2}\right)$
$\therefore \begin{aligned} \frac{1 / f_a}{1 / f_l} & =\frac{\left(\mu_g / \mu_a-1\right)}{\left(\frac{\mu_g}{\mu_l}-1\right)} \\ \frac{f_l}{f_a} & =\frac{\left(\frac{3 / 2}{1}-1\right)}{\left(\frac{3 / 2}{\mu_l}-1\right)} \\ \frac{0.375}{0.15} & =\frac{1 / 2}{\left(\frac{3}{2 \mu_l}-1\right)} \\ \left(\frac{3}{2 \mu_l}-1\right) & =\frac{1 / 2}{5 / 2} \\ \frac{3}{2 \mu_l}-1 & =\frac{1}{5} \\ \frac{3}{2 \mu_l} & =\frac{1}{5}+1 \Rightarrow \frac{3}{2 \mu_l}=\frac{6}{5} \\ \mu_l & =\frac{5}{4}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The dispersive powers of the materials of two lenses forming an achromatic combination are in the ratio of $4: 3$. Effective focal length of the two lenses is $+60 \mathrm{~cm}$ then the focal lengths of the lenses should be
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} P=P_1+P_2 & =\frac{q}{f_1}+\frac{q}{f_2} \\ \frac{1}{60} & =\frac{q}{f_1}+\frac{q}{f_2} \\ \frac{1}{60} & =\frac{f_1+f_2}{f_1 f_2}\end{aligned}$
According to question,
$\frac{\omega_1}{\omega_2}=\frac{f_1}{f_2} \Rightarrow \frac{f_1}{f_2}=\frac{-4}{3}$
or
$f_1=-\frac{4}{3} f_2$
From Eq. (i)
$\frac{1}{60}=\frac{\frac{4}{3} f_2+f_2}{\left(f_2\right)^2 \frac{4}{3}} \Rightarrow \frac{-\frac{1}{3}}{-\frac{4}{3} f_2}$
$\begin{aligned} f_2 & =15 \mathrm{~cm} \\ \therefore \quad f_1 & =-\frac{4}{3} \times 15 \\ & =-20 \mathrm{~cm}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A transparent solid cylindrical rod has a refractive index of 23. It is surrounded by air. A light ray is incident at the midpoint of one end of the rod as shown in the figure. The incident angle θ for which the light ray grazes along the wall of the rod is
questions.solution:
From Snell's law,
μairsinθ=μcylidersin 90°–θC
⇒1×sinθ=23×cosθC
But, sin⁡θC=1μcylinder=32
Therefore cos⁡θC=12
⇒sinθ=23.12=13
⇒θ=sin–1⁡13
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A convex lens has its radii of curvature equal. The focal length of the lens is $f$. If it is divided vertically into two identical plano-convex lenses by cutting it, then the focal length of the plano-convex lens is ( $\mu=$ the refractive index of the material of the lens)
questions.solution:
The given, $R_1=R, R_2=-R$ $$ f=F $$
Lens Maker's formula $$ \begin{aligned} \frac{1}{F} & =(\mu-1)\left[\frac{1}{R_1}-\frac{1}{R_2}\right] \\ \frac{1}{f} & =(\mu-1)\left[\frac{1}{R}+\frac{1}{R}\right] \\ f & =\frac{R}{2(\mu-1)} \\ R & =2 f(\mu-1) \end{aligned} $$ Now, it is divided vertically into two identical plano convex lens
$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{f^{\prime}}=(\mu-1)\left[\frac{1}{R_1}-\frac{1}{R_2}\right] \\ & \frac{1}{f_1}=(\mu-1)\left[\frac{1}{R}-\frac{1}{\infty}\right] \quad\left[\because R_1=R, R_2=\infty\right] \\ & f_1=\frac{R}{(\mu-1)} \\ & f_1=\frac{2 f(\mu-1)}{(\mu-1)} \\ & f_1=2 f \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An image is formed at a distance of $100 \mathrm{~cm}$ from the glass surface when light from point source in air falls on a spherical glass surface with refractive index 1.5. The distance of the light source from the glass surface is $100 \mathrm{~cm}$. The radius of curvature is
questions.solution:
The refraction formula is $\frac{\mu_2}{v}-\frac{\mu_1}{u}=\frac{\mu_2-\mu_1}{R}$
$\begin{aligned} & \mu_2=1.5, \mu_1 \text { (air) }=1, u=-100 \mathrm{~cm}, v=100 \mathrm{~cm} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{1.5}{100}+\frac{1}{100}=\frac{1.5-1}{R} \Rightarrow \frac{25}{100}=\frac{0.5}{R} \Rightarrow R=20 \mathrm{~cm}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: When an object is placed at a distance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> from a mirror, the magnification is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>m</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math>. But the magnification becomes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>m</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, when the object is moved <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> farther away with respect to the earlier position and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>m</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><msub><mi>m</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mtext>,</mtext></math> then find the focal length of the mirror and what type of mirror it is?
options: 20 cm, convex
20 cm, concave
10 cm, convex
10 cm, concave
questions.solution:
m=-vu=ff-u

so,m1=ff--25=ff+25

Similarly,m2=ff+40

andm1m2=4

∴f+40f+25=4

               f=-20 cm
 
Negative sign shows that the mirror is concave.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A thin prism $\mathrm{P}_{1}$ with angle $4^{\circ}$ and made from glass of refractive index $1 \cdot 54$ is
combined with another thin prism $\mathrm{P}_{2}$ made from glass of refractive index $1 \cdot 72$ to
produce dispersion without deviation. The angle of prism for $\mathrm{P}_{2}$ is
questions.solution:
For thin prism the angle of deviation $\delta=\mathrm{A}(\mathrm{n}-1)$
$\therefore \delta_{1}=\delta_{2} \quad$ for no deviation
$\therefore A_{1}\left(n_{1}-1\right)=A_{2}\left(n_{2}-1\right)$
$4(1.54-1)=\mathrm{A}_{2}(1.72-1)$
$\therefore 4 \times 0.54=\mathrm{A}_{2} \times 0.72$
$\mathrm{A}_{2}=\frac{4 \times 0.54}{0.72}=3^{\circ}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A light ray incidents normally on one surface of an equilateral prism. The angle of deviation of the light ray is (refractive index of the material of the prism $=\sqrt{2}$ )
questions.solution: Given, $i=0^{\circ}$ So, $$ \begin{aligned} \frac{\sin i}{\sin r_1} & =\sqrt{2} \\ \frac{\sin 0^{\circ}}{\sin r_1} & =\sqrt{2} \\ \sin r_1 & =0 \text { or } r_1=0 \end{aligned} $$ Now, $\frac{\sin r_2}{\sin e}=\frac{1}{\mu}$ $$ \Rightarrow \quad \sin e=\sqrt{2} \sin r_2 $$ But, $\quad r_1+r_2=60^{\circ}$ So, $$ \begin{aligned} \sin e & =\sqrt{2} \sin 60^{\circ} \\ & =\sqrt{2} \times \sqrt{3} / 2>1 \end{aligned} $$ So, light is incidenting at more than critical angle and totally internally reflected. $$ \begin{aligned} \therefore \text { Deviation angle } & =(i+e)-\left(r_1+r_2\right) \\ & =0-60^{\circ}=60^{\circ}(\text { in magnitude }) \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The focal length of an equi-convex lens is greater than the radius of curvature of any of the surfaces. Then the refractive index of the material of the lens is
options: greater than zero but less than 1.5
questions.solution:
Focal length of lens,
$\frac{1}{f}=(\mu-1)\left(\frac{1}{R_1}-\frac{1}{R_2}\right)$
For equi-convex lens,
$\begin{aligned}
R_1 & =+R, R_2=-R \\
\frac{1}{f} & =(\mu-1)\left(\frac{1}{R}-\frac{1}{-R}\right) \\
\frac{1}{f} & =(\mu-1)\left(\frac{2}{R}\right) \\
f & =\frac{R}{2(\mu-1)} \\
f < R, \text { so, } 2(\mu-1) & < 1 \\
(\mu-1) & < \frac{1}{2} \\
\text { i.e. } \quad(\mu-1) & < 0.5 \\
\mu & < 1.5
\end{aligned}$
Focal length of convex lens is positive. So, $\mu$ cannot be negative, hence $\mu$ should be greater than zero but less than 1.5.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A thin glass prism of <math><mi>μ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1.5</mn></math> is immersed in water of <math><mi>μ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1.33</mn></math>. The ratio of deviation of the ray in water to that in air for the same prism is
questions.solution:
In air δ=1.5-1A=A2
In water δ=μgμw-1A=1.51.33-1A=A8
δin water δin air=14
We know that, δin air=aμg-1A and δin water=wμg-1A.
δwδa=μgμw-1μ9-1
δwδa=3243-132-1
δwδa=98-132-1
δwδa=1812=14
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A prism of refractive index $\mu$ and angle $A$ is placed in the minimum deviation position. If the angle of minimum deviation is $A$, then the value of $A$ in terms of $\mu$ is :
questions.solution:
Refractive index of material of prism
$\mu=\frac{\sin \left(\frac{A+\delta_m}{2}\right)}{\sin \frac{A}{2}}$
but $\quad \delta_m=A$
$\mu=\frac{\sin \frac{A+A}{2}}{\sin \frac{A}{2}}$
$\mu=\frac{\sin A}{\sin A / 2}$
$\mu=\frac{2 \sin A / 2 \cos A / 2}{\sin A / 2}$
$\cos \frac{A}{2}=\frac{\mu}{2}$
$\frac{A}{2}=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{\mu}{2}\right)$
$A=2 \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{\mu}{2}\right)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A plano-convex lens of focal length 10 cm is silvered at its plane face. The distance d at which an object must be placed in order to get its image on itself is
questions.solution:
If the object and the image coincide in the shown combination in the question, it will behave like a converging mirror. So, applying mirror formula to the combination
1f=1v+1u  ...1

where, u, v are the positions of the object and image, respectively and f is the focal length.

Here,

u=v=-d,

Hence, substituting in equation (1),
1f=1-d+1-d
     =-2d

For the combination,
1f=2fl+1fm  ...2

where, fl, fm are the focal lengths of the lens and mirror, respectively.


fl=10 cm and fm=∞ as silvered surface is plane mirror (given).

Substituting in equation (2),
2d=210+1∞
⇒d=10 cm
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The objective and eyepiece of an astronomical telescope are double convex lenses with refractive index 1.5. When the telescope is adjusted to infinity, the separation between the two lenses is $16 \mathrm{~cm}$. If the space between the lenses is now filled with water and again telescope is adjusted for infinity, then the present separation between the lenses is
questions.solution:
From the lens maker formula
$\begin{array}{cc} & \frac{\mu_3}{v}-\frac{\mu_1}{u}=\frac{\mu_2-\mu_1}{R_1}+\frac{\mu_3-\mu_2}{R_2} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \frac{4}{3 f_o{ }^{\prime}}-\frac{1}{\infty}=\frac{1.5-1}{R}+\frac{\frac{4}{3}-1.5}{-R} \\ f_o{ }^{\prime}=R=2 f\end{array}$
Now, length $=f_o{ }^{\prime}+f_e{ }^{\prime}$
$\begin{aligned} & =2 f_o+2 f_e=2\left(f_o+f_e\right) \\ & =2(L)=2(16)=32 \mathrm{~cm}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: With the help of a telescope that has an objective of diameter $200 \mathrm{~cm}$, it is proved that light of wavelengths of the order of $6400 Å$ coming from a star can be easily resolved. Then, the limit of resolution is
questions.solution:
The limit of resolution of a telescope is
$$
\theta=\frac{1.22 \lambda}{d}
$$
where, $\lambda=$ wavelength of light rays
$d$ = aperture of objective lens of telescope.
Here, $\lambda=6400 Å=6400 \times 10^{-10} \mathrm{~m}$
$$
d=200 \mathrm{~cm}=2 \mathrm{~m}
$$
Substitution of values gives
$$
\begin{aligned}
\theta & =\frac{1.22 \times 6400 \times 10^{-10}}{2} \\
& =\frac{1.22 \times 64 \times 10^{-8}}{2} \\
& =1.22 \times 32 \times 10^{-8} \\
& =39 \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{rad}
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: When light enters glass from vacuum then the wavelength of light
questions.solution:
When a light enters glass form vacuum, then the wavelnght of light decrease.
This is because the speed of light in glass in less than that in the vacuum.
From Snell's law the refractive index in a medium is given by.
$\mu=\frac{c}{v}$
The glass is of higher refractive index than vaccum, so speed of light decrease and as teh speed of light is drectly proportional to the wavelenght ( $c \equiv v \lambda$ ), So the wavelenght also be decrease.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An object is fixed at the bottom of a vessel and water is filled in the vessel upto a height of $10 \mathrm{~cm}$. A plane mirror is placed at a height of $7 \mathrm{~cm}$ from the surface of water in such a way that its reflecting surface faces the water. The distance of the image from the mirror is (Refractive index of water, $n=1.33$ )
questions.solution:
According to the question,
the apparent distance of object from the mirror, distance of image from the mirror, $d$ $$ =7 \mathrm{~cm}+\text { apparent depth } $$ $$ \begin{aligned} \because \text { Apparent depth } & =\frac{\text { Real depth }}{\mu} \\ & =\frac{10}{1.33} \quad(\because \mu=1.33 \text {, given }) \end{aligned} $$ Hence, $d=7 \mathrm{~cm}+\frac{10}{1.33}=14.5 \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The two surfaces of a biconvex lens has same radii of curvatures. This lens is made of glass of refractive index 1.5 and has a focal length $10 \mathrm{~cm}$ in air. The lens is cut into two equal halves along a plane perpendicular to its principal axis to yield two plano-convex lenses. The two pieces are glued such that the convex surfaces touch each other. If this combination lens is immersed in water (refractive index $=$ $4 / 3$ ), its focal length (in $\mathrm{cm}$ ) is :
questions.solution:
If $a$ lens of focal length $f$ is divided into two equal parts as shown in figure (1) and each has a focal length $f^{\prime}$ then $\frac{1}{f}=\frac{1}{f^{\prime}}+\frac{1}{f^{\prime}} \quad \text { i.e., } f^{\prime}=2 f$ i.e., each part will have focal length $2 f$. Now if these parts are put in contact as in figure (2), then resultant focal length of the combination will be ' $\frac{1}{F}=\frac{1}{2 f}+\frac{1}{2 f} \text { i.e., } F=f \quad \text { (initial value) }$
For this combination, $\frac{1}{F}=\left({ }_a \mu_g-1\right)\left(\frac{1}{R_1}-\frac{1}{R_2}\right)$ $\ldots$ (i) Now, if this combination is immersed in liquid, then $\frac{1}{F^{\prime}}=\left({ }_l \mu_g-1\right)\left(\frac{1}{R_1}-\frac{1}{R_2}\right)$ $\ldots$ (ii) $\therefore \frac{F^{\prime}}{f}=\frac{\left({ }_a \mu_g-1\right)}{\left.{ }_l \mu_g-1\right)}=\frac{(1.5-1)}{\left(\frac{\frac{3}{2}}{\frac{4}{3}}-1\right)}$ or $\frac{F^{\prime}}{f}=\frac{0.5}{\left(\frac{9}{8}-1\right)}=0.5 \times 8$ $\therefore F^{\prime}=0.5 \times 8 \times 10=40 \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A point source of light B placed at a distance L in front of the centre of a mirror of width d hung vertically on a wall. A man walks in front of the mirror along a line parallel to the mirror at a distance 2L from it as shown in figure. The greatest distance over which he can see the image of the light source in the mirror is :
questions.solution:
Consider the ray diagram of the situation given in the problem, we get

HI=AS=d

DB=CD=d2

Since, 

Since, AH=2AD

∴  GH=2CD=2d2=d

Similarly, IJ=d

 ∴  GJ=GH+HI+IJ=d+d+d=3d
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A ray of light is incident on the surface of a glass plate of refractive index \(\sqrt{3}\) at the polarising angle. The angle of refraction of the ray is
questions.solution:
Given, refractive index of glass plate, \(\mu=\sqrt{3}\)
Polarising angle \(\theta\) of incident light is given as,
\(\mu=\tan \theta\)
or \(\sqrt{3}=\tan \theta \text { or } \theta=\tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{3})\)
\(\therefore\) angle of incident, \(\theta=60^{\circ}\) \([\because \tan 60=\sqrt{3}]\)
refractive index is given as,
\(\mu=\frac{\sin i}{\sin r}\)
or \(\sqrt{3} \sin r=\sin 60^{\circ} \quad \left[\because i=\theta=60^{\circ}\right]\)
or \(\sqrt{3} \sin r=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \text { or } \sin r=\frac{1}{2}\)
or \(r=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) \Rightarrow r=30^{\circ}\)
Thus, the angle of refraction of the ray is \(30^{\circ}\).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
A thin convex lens of focal length $f$ made of crown glass is immersed in a liquid of refractive index $\mu_I\left(\mu_I>\mu_c\right)$, where $\mu_c$ is the refractive index of the crown glass.
The convex lens now is
options: a convex lens of longer focal length
a convex lens of shorter focal length
a convex lens of focal length $\left(\mu_c-\mu_1\right\} f$
questions.solution: When the convex lens of focal length $f$ made of crown glass immersed in liquid of refractive index $\mu_f\left(\mu_l>\mu_c\right)$, here $\mu_c$ is the refractive index of crown glass, thus from lens maker's formula, we will get focal length of the lens as negative $\left(\because \mu_e>u_c\right)$. Therefore, the convex lens will behave like a concave lens. So, it will be a divergent lens.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A convex lens is cut into two parts in different ways that are arranged in four manners, as shown. Which arrangement will give maximum optical power?
questions.solution:
We know that power is inversely proportional to focal length for a given lens. The point at which the parallel beam converges after passing through the lens is the focus. So highly convergent lens will have less focal length therefore maximum power.
Among the given pictures converging power is high for this one as there are two converging lenses with principal axis coinciding.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Light of wavelength <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>300</mn><mo> </mo><mi>nm</mi></math> in medium <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> enters into medium <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> through a plane surface. If the frequency of light is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>14</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi>Hz</mi></math> and the ratio of speed in medium <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> to that is medium <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>4</mn><mn>5</mn></mfrac></math>, the absolute refractive index of medium <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> is,
questions.solution:
Absolute refractive index of medium A, μA=cνAλ=3×1085×1014×300×10-9=2

Refractive index is inversely proportional to the velocity of light.

⇒μBμA=vAvB=45

⇒μB=μA45=85

⇒μB=1.6
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: In a compound microscope, the focal lengths of two lenses are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext>1.5 cm</mtext></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext>6.25 cm</mtext></math>. An object is placed at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext>2 cm</mtext></math> from objective and the final image is formed at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext>25 cm</mtext></math> from eye lens. The distance between the two lenses is

f0=1.5cm,⁡fe=6.25cm,⁡u0=-D=-25 cm

11.5=1v0-1-2

⇒ 1v0=11.5-12

or v0=6 cm

questions.q: A concave lens of glass of refractive index <math><mn>1.5</mn></math>, has both surfaces of the same radius of curvature <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math>. On immersion in a medium of refractive index <math><mn>1.75</mn></math>, it will behave as a
options: convergent lens of focal length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.5</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math>
convergent lens of focal length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.0</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math>
divergent lens of focal length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.5</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math>
divergent lens of focal length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.0</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math>
questions.solution:
Focal length of equiconcave lens

f=-R2μ-1

Where μ=μlensμmedium

Therefore focal length in the medium

fmed=-R21.51.75-1

fmed=+R20.251.75

fmed=+3.5R

Positive sign implies convergent sign.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: To a bird in air, a fish in water appears to be at $30 \mathrm{~cm}$ from the surface. If refractive index of water with respect to air is $\frac{4}{3}$, the real distance of bird from the surface is
questions.solution: The correct option is (C). Consider the labeled figure. The fish appears at an apparent depth h๘ while the real depth is $h$. In triangle $\mathrm{OPF}: \tan (\mathrm{i})=\frac{\mathrm{P}}{\mathrm{h}} \simeq \sin (\mathrm{i})$ In triangle OPA: $\tan (\mathrm{r})=\frac{\mathrm{P}}{\mathrm{h}^{\prime}} \simeq \sin (\mathrm{r})$ For small angles $\theta, \operatorname{tam} \theta \simeq \sin \theta$ can be taken. Using smell's law of refraction: $1 \times \sin (\mathrm{r})=\mu \times \sin (\mathrm{i})$ Inserting expressions for $\sin (\mathrm{i})$ and $\sin (\mathrm{r}) 1 \times \frac{\mathrm{P}}{\mathrm{h}^{\prime}}=\mu \times \frac{\mathrm{P}}{\mathrm{h}}$ $\mathrm{h}=\mu \mathrm{h}^{\prime}$ Given, $\mathrm{h}^{\prime}=30 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $\mu=\frac{4}{3}$, therefore real depth $\mathrm{h}=40 \mathrm{~cm}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A half spherical glass lens with refractive index 1.5 is placed in a liquid with refractive index of 1.3 (see following figure). The radius of the half spherical lens is $10 \mathrm{~cm}$. A parallel beam of light travelling in the liquid is refracted by the glass lens. Then the absolute value of the position of the image from the centre of the glass lens will be
questions.solution:
Focal length of the lens,
$$
\frac{1}{f}=\left(\frac{n_2}{n_1}-1\right)\left(\frac{1}{R_1}-\frac{1}{R_2}\right)
$$
Here, refractive index for glass lens, $n_2=1.5$
Refractive index for water, $n_1=1.3$
Radius of curvature, $R_1=10 \mathrm{~cm}$,
$$
R_2=\infty
$$
Putting these values, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
\frac{1}{f} & =\left(\frac{1.5}{1.3}-1\right)\left(\frac{1}{10}-\frac{1}{\infty}\right) \\
\frac{1}{f} & =\frac{015}{10} \\
\Rightarrow \quad f & =65 \mathrm{~cm}
\end{aligned}
$$
Now, paralleI beam of light will converge at the focus of lens, so image position $v=65 \mathrm{~cm}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A thin biconvex lens of refractive index 32 is placed on a horizontal plane mirror as shown in the figure. The space between the lens and the mirror is then filled with water of refractive index 43. It is found that when a point object is placed 15 cm above the lens on its principal axis, the object coincides with its own image. On repeating with another liquid, the object and the image again coincide at a distance 25 cm from the lens. Calculate 10 times refractive index of the liquid.
questions.solution:
Let R be the radius of curvature of both the surfaces of the equiconvex lens. In the first case

Let f1 be the focal length of planoconcave lens of index μ1. The focal length of the combined lens system will be given by
                1 F = 1 f 1 + 1 f 2



          = μ 1 - 1 1 R - 1 - R + μ 2 - 1 1 - R - 1 ∞

         = 3 2 - 1 2 R + 4 3 - 1 - 1 R

         =1R-13R=23R or  F=3R2


Now, image coincides with the object when ray of light retraces its path or it falls normally on the plane mirror. This is possible only when object is at focus of the lens system.

Hence,  F=15 cm      ( Distance of object = 15 cm).

or         3R2=15 cm or R=10 cm

In the second case, let μ be the refractive index of the liquid-filled between lens and mirror and let F' be the focal length of new lens system. Then,



1F'=μ1-11R-1-R+μ-11-R-1∞

or,   1F'=32-12R-μ-1R

or,   1F'=1R-μ-1R=2-μR

 F'=R2-μ=102-μ    R=10 cm


Now, the image coincides with object when it is placed at 25 cm distance.

Hence,   F ′ = 2 5

or,    102-μ=25  or   50-25μ=10 or 25μ=40

   ∴ μ=4025=1·6   or    μ=1·6
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A thin converging lens of focal length $f=25 \mathrm{~cm}$ forms the image of an object on a screen placed at a distance of $75 \mathrm{~cm}$ from the lens. The screen is moved closer to the lens by a distance of $25 \mathrm{~cm}$. The distance through which the object has to be shifted, so that its image on the screen in sharp again is
questions.solution:
According to the first condition,
$$ \begin{aligned} f & =25 \mathrm{~cm}, v=75 \mathrm{~cm} \\ u & =? \\ \frac{1}{f} & =\frac{1}{v}-\frac{1}{u} \\ \frac{1}{25} & =\frac{1}{75}-\frac{1}{u} \\ \frac{1}{u} & =\frac{1}{75}-\frac{1}{25} \\ \frac{1}{u} & =\frac{1-3}{75} \\ u & =-\frac{75}{2}=-37.5 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned} $$ According to the second condition
$$ \begin{aligned} v_1=50 \mathrm{~cm}, f & =25 \mathrm{~cm}, u_1=? \\ \frac{1}{f} & =\frac{1}{v_1}-\frac{1}{u_1} \\ \frac{1}{25} & =\frac{1}{50}-\frac{1}{u_1} \\ \frac{1}{u_1} & =\frac{1}{50}-\frac{1}{25} \\ \Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{u_1} & =\frac{1-2}{50} \\ \Rightarrow \quad u_1 & =-50 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned} $$ So, the screen is sharp again is $$ \begin{aligned} \Delta u & =u_1-u \\ & =50-37.5 \\ & =12.5 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
